stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of vehicle speed using a speedometer. The measurements have a mean of 85.0 km/h and a standard deviation of 10.4 km/h. However, calibration tests reveal that a speedometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.2% and 7.3%. Estimate the true vehicle speed and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 85.0 km/h. After correcting for the 4.8% bias: ≈ 81.1 km/h. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.2%-7.3%) and sampling variability: [73.8, 88.6]. Point estimate: 81.1 km/h.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
